reasoning: Direct definitional consequence — `s.len() == 0` branch returns `zero`. Standard base case.

Folding an infinite set silently returns `zero` with no indication of failure. This is a design choice that could mask bugs — callers passing an infinite set get a valid-looking result instead of an obviously wrong one.
Direct consequence of `set_fold` returning `Set::empty()` (the zero) on an empty input set. Expected behavior.
The ensures is a trivial tautology (`x == x`). It proves nothing about order-dependence or commutativity.
